steps.title: Check the two equations
steps.content:
The second equation is just $3$ times the first:
$$15x+21y=33$$
$$5x+7y=11$$So both equations describe the same line.

steps.title: Solve for y
steps.content:
Use the first equation and let $x=r$:
$$5r+7y=11$$
$$7y=11-5r$$
$$y=-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{11}{7}$$

steps.title: Write the point
steps.content: For each real number $r$, the point is $(r,-\frac{5r}{7}+\frac{11}{7})$. The answer is A.
